briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware firm Synapse, raising serious questions about consumer protections in banking-as-a-service models.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing interest in leveraging open finance data for consumer financial services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what that means for incumbents and challengers alike.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit card products into increasingly niche and unconventional territory, disrupting the traditional rewards and lending landscape.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada officially labelled screen scraping as insecure and committed to a target timeline for launching an open banking framework, a foundational moment for Canadian fintech regulation.

- **[How new SR&ED changes could supercharge Canadian hard tech and manufacturing startups](https://betakit.com/how-new-sred-changes-could-supercharge-canadian-hard-tech-and-manufacturing-startups/)** — *BetaKit* — Proposed reforms to Canada's Scientific Research and Experimental Development tax credit program could significantly boost funding and competitiveness for Canadian tech and manufacturing startups.

- **[BDC launches $150-million life sciences fund](https://betakit.com/bdc-launches-150-million-life-sciences-fund/)** — *BetaKit* — The Business Development Bank of Canada has unveiled a $150-million fund targeting life sciences ventures, expanding its role as a key source of venture capital for Canadian innovators.

- **[How the war in the Middle East is already impacting mortgage rates in Canada](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/iran-war-mortgage-rates-canada-9.7152182?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The ongoing US-Iran conflict is driving up energy prices and bond yields, putting upward pressure on Canadian mortgage rates and household financial stress.

- **[Blackline Safety to go private in $850-million deal](https://betakit.com/blackline-safety-to-go-private-in-850-million-usd-deal/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian connected safety company Blackline Safety has agreed to a landmark $850-million USD go-private transaction, marking one of the largest Canadian tech deals of the year.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This critical resource cataloguing the darker side of fintech — from predatory lending to consumer harm — is a timely reminder of why thoughtful regulation and financial inclusion must remain central to ecosystem building in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Canada's spotty support for innovation is grounding its space ambitions](https://betakit.com/canadas-spotty-support-for-innovation-is-grounding-its-space-ambitions/)** — *BetaKit* — A broader critique of Canada's inconsistent innovation funding landscape that resonates with Atlantic Canadian fintech builders navigating limited venture capital access and federal support gaps.
